timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Temple and palace accounting spreads
description: Urban economies in Mesopotamia use measured grain, silver, and written accounts to standardize value and obligation.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Standard silver weights circulate
description: Weight-based silver and other commodities become common stores of value in Near Eastern trade.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Cowrie shells become a major currency
description: Cowrie shells are widely used as exchange media across parts of Asia and Africa because they are durable and portable.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: True coinage emerges in Lydia
description: Stamped metal coins appear in western Anatolia, creating a portable and state-backed medium of exchange.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Coinage spreads across the Greek world
description: Greek city-states adopt coinage, helping connect trade, taxation, and military payment.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Rome introduces the denarius
description: The Roman denarius becomes one of antiquity's most influential silver currencies and spreads through imperial commerce.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Tang China expands paper instruments
description: Chinese merchants and officials increasingly use paper-based instruments that foreshadow large-scale paper money.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Song China issues state paper money
description: The Song state institutionalizes government-issued paper money, a major break from purely metallic currency.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Mongol rule broadens paper currency use
description: Yuan authorities promote paper money over a vast Eurasian empire, spreading awareness of fiduciary money.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Barcelona founds the Taula de Canvi
description: One of Europe's early public banks helps institutionalize deposit banking and public finance.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Sweden establishes a modern central bank
description: The Bank of Sweden becomes a model for later central banks and modern monetary management.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Bank of England is founded
description: The new bank becomes a durable model for state finance, note issuance, and central banking.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Britain commits to gold convertibility
description: Post-Napoleonic reform helps anchor sterling to gold and strengthens the modern gold-standard system.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Gold standard globalizes
description: Germany, France, and the United States join Britain in adopting gold-based monetary arrangements.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: The Federal Reserve is created
description: The United States establishes a central bank to stabilize credit, banking, and the money supply.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Britain leaves the gold standard
description: Financial crisis during the Great Depression accelerates the breakdown of classical gold-based money.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Diners Club launches a universal charge card
description: A card usable at multiple businesses marks a decisive step toward mass consumer credit.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: BankAmericard expands bank card culture
description: Bank of America launches the card later known as Visa, helping normalize revolving consumer credit.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Australia popularizes polymer banknotes
description: Polymer notes spread as a durable, anti-counterfeiting alternative to traditional paper currency.

timeline_description: A history of money from early commodity exchange and coinage to central banking, credit cards, and cryptocurrency.
title: Bitcoin launches decentralized digital money
description: Bitcoin introduces blockchain-based currency without a central issuing authority and reshapes debate over money itself.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Mesopotamian numeral systems mature
description: Administrators in Mesopotamia develop place-based numerical notation for accounting, measurement, and astronomy.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Egyptian practical geometry develops
description: Surveying, construction, and taxation drive early geometric procedures in ancient Egypt.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Babylonian tablets solve quadratic problems
description: Clay tablets show sophisticated work with algebraic procedures, ratios, and area problems.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Greek deductive mathematics takes shape
description: Thinkers associated with the Pythagorean tradition link number, proof, and abstract reasoning.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Euclid systematizes geometry
description: The Elements organizes axioms, definitions, and proofs into a durable model of mathematical method.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Archimedes advances measurement
description: Archimedes refines methods for area, volume, and approximation that anticipate later calculus.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Chinese mathematical classics circulate
description: Texts later gathered as The Nine Chapters codify arithmetic, extraction of roots, and linear methods.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Brahmagupta states rules for zero
description: Indian mathematics gives zero a clear arithmetic role and formalizes operations with positive and negative numbers.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: al-Khwarizmi founds algebraic tradition
description: Arabic mathematical writing systematizes equation solving and helps transmit Hindu-Arabic numerals westward.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Latin Europe absorbs Arabic mathematics
description: Translation movements bring algebra, numerals, and classical Greek works into European scholarship.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Fibonacci popularizes Hindu-Arabic numerals
description: Liber Abaci helps spread positional notation and commercial arithmetic in Latin Europe.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Descartes unites algebra and geometry
description: Analytic geometry lets equations describe curves and reshapes mathematical problem solving.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Pascal and Fermat launch probability theory
description: Correspondence on games of chance establishes a new mathematical field with wide practical uses.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Newton develops calculus
description: Methods of fluxions open a new way to analyze change, motion, and accumulation.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Leibniz introduces calculus notation
description: Leibniz's notation for differentials and integrals becomes the dominant language of calculus.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Gauss modernizes number theory
description: Disquisitiones Arithmeticae helps turn number theory into a rigorous modern discipline.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Non-Euclidean geometry breaks classical monopoly
description: Lobachevsky shows that coherent geometries can be built without Euclid's parallel postulate.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Turing formalizes computability
description: The abstract Turing machine clarifies what it means for a procedure to be algorithmic.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Shannon founds information theory
description: A mathematical theory of communication links probability, coding, and the digital age.

timeline_description: A history of mathematics from ancient numeral systems and geometry to modern computation and AI-era algorithms.
title: Transformers showcase modern mathematical AI
description: Attention-based neural network design demonstrates how linear algebra, optimization, and probability underpin state-of-the-art AI.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Royal inscriptions advertise power
description: Monumental inscriptions in early states present rulers as divinely favored and politically legitimate.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Ramesses II publicizes Kadesh
description: Egyptian monuments depict the Battle of Kadesh as a triumph, shaping royal memory beyond the battlefield.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Classical rhetoric becomes political weapon
description: Greek public life ties persuasion to assembly politics, civic identity, and war mobilization.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Roman coinage spreads imperial messaging
description: Images and slogans on coins carry political narratives through the Roman world.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Imperial Christianity uses mass symbolism
description: State-backed religious imagery, liturgy, and architecture reinforce new imperial identities.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Crusade preaching mobilizes Europe
description: Papal calls and sermons frame war in moral and sacred terms for mass audiences.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Printing press transforms persuasion
description: Movable type dramatically expands the speed, scale, and repeatability of polemical messaging.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Reformation pamphlet wars erupt
description: Lutheran and Catholic camps weaponize cheap print, images, and vernacular texts.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Congregatio de Propaganda Fide is founded
description: The Catholic Church institutionalizes the term that later becomes 'propaganda.'

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Revolutionary pamphlets shape public opinion
description: Atlantic revolutions show how mass persuasion can mobilize political rupture.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: French revolutionary festivals stage ideology
description: Ceremony, iconography, and print create a new political culture of mass symbolism.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Telegraph and illustrated press accelerate messaging
description: Industrial communications allow governments and newspapers to circulate narratives faster than ever.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: World War I launches industrial propaganda
description: Belligerent states build ministries, posters, films, and atrocity narratives for total war.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: The Creel Committee coordinates U.S. messaging
description: The United States creates a formal wartime propaganda apparatus spanning film, print, and public speaking.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Nazi propaganda ministry centralizes media
description: Joseph Goebbels uses radio, film, rallies, and censorship to build a totalitarian information order.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Radio propaganda shapes the Spanish Civil War
description: Competing factions use modern broadcasting to frame ideology and international support.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: World War II propaganda becomes global multimedia
description: Governments rely on posters, cinema, broadcasting, and patriotic messaging on a massive scale.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Cold War propaganda saturates everyday life
description: Superpowers use broadcasting, cultural diplomacy, and education to compete for global legitimacy.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Internet platforms transform information warfare
description: Web forums, social media, and algorithmic distribution make propaganda more decentralized and rapid.

timeline_description: A history of propaganda from royal inscriptions and religious campaigns to mass media, social platforms, and deepfakes.
title: Deepfakes enter the propaganda toolkit
description: Synthetic media expands the persuasive possibilities of disinformation in the social media age.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Clay-tablet archives appear in early cities
description: Administrations in Mesopotamia preserve transactions, laws, and inventories in organized written records.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Temple record rooms expand
description: Egyptian and Mesopotamian institutions maintain dedicated spaces for taxation, ritual, and estate records.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Ashurbanipal assembles a royal archive-library
description: Nineveh preserves large bodies of cuneiform texts and state documents in systematic form.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Hellenistic states professionalize record custody
description: Expanding bureaucracies depend on archives to administer taxation, diplomacy, and land rights.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Roman tabularia support imperial administration
description: Official record houses become integral to governance, law, and citizenship in Roman public life.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Islamic chanceries systematize documentary rule
description: Growing caliphal states depend on written registers, tax records, and administrative correspondence.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Monastic scriptoria preserve documentary culture
description: Religious houses copy charters, chronicles, and legal records that survive political fragmentation.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Royal chancery systems expand in Europe
description: Medieval monarchies increasingly depend on written enrollments, seals, and stored state papers.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: English parishes begin mandatory registers
description: Regular recording of baptisms, marriages, and burials creates durable local archival series.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: The Vatican Secret Archives are formalized
description: The papacy centralizes and preserves a major documentary record of church governance.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Revolutionary France creates national archives
description: Modern public archival administration takes shape as records are claimed for the nation.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Britain establishes the Public Record Office
description: Centralized archival custody becomes a hallmark of modern government accountability.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Microphotography enters archival practice
description: Miniaturized reproduction opens new possibilities for preservation and access.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: The U.S. National Archives is founded
description: A federal archival agency is created to preserve and make accessible government records.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Machine-readable records become archival concern
description: Governments and institutions begin preserving records created in electronic formats.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Records management links current and permanent files
description: Archivists formalize life-cycle thinking for appraisal, retention, and preservation.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: UNESCO launches Memory of the World
description: An international program begins protecting documentary heritage from loss and neglect.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Web archiving emerges as a major task
description: Archivists confront the fragility of born-digital culture and the speed of online disappearance.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: UNESCO expands archival digitization
description: New digitization efforts widen public access to documentary heritage previously limited to reading rooms.

timeline_description: A history of archives and record-keeping from clay tablets to national archives, digitization, and web preservation.
title: Memory of the World register continues to grow
description: UNESCO adds new documentary heritage items, underscoring the global turn toward preservation and access.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Early planned gardens appear in Egypt and Mesopotamia
description: Irrigation and enclosure enable gardens that combine food production, shade, and symbolic display.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Detailed Egyptian garden plans survive
description: A garden plan from Thebes shows formal layout, ponds, vines, and tree-lined avenues.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Persian royal gardens refine enclosure
description: Walled gardens combine irrigation, geometry, and royal symbolism in the wider Iranian world.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Hellenistic gardens blend pleasure and learning
description: Greek and later Hellenistic elites integrate walks, sculpture, and cultivated nature.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Roman villas popularize ornamental domestic gardens
description: Courtyards, topiary, and water features become defining elements of elite household design.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Islamic garden traditions expand
description: Gardens across the Islamic world emphasize water, enclosure, scent, and paradise imagery.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Monastic herb plots anchor medieval gardening
description: Cloister gardens preserve medicinal, culinary, and devotional plant culture.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Secular castle gardens spread in Europe
description: Gardens move beyond cloisters into aristocratic domestic landscapes.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Pisa founds one of the first botanical gardens
description: Universities create living collections for the study of medicinal plants and natural history.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Padua expands the botanical garden model
description: The botanical garden becomes a formal institution of research, teaching, and collection.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Baroque formal gardens dominate court culture
description: Axial design, terraces, fountains, and disciplined geometry turn gardens into displays of power.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Versailles begins its monumental transformation
description: French court gardening reaches its most famous expression in the landscapes of Louis XIV.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: The English landscape garden rejects rigid formality
description: Designers favor rolling lawns, lakes, and orchestrated naturalism over strict symmetry.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Gardenesque planting celebrates specimen collecting
description: Plant hunters and nurseries encourage display of diverse species in curated layouts.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Public parks democratize designed greenery
description: Urban reformers make landscaped green space a civic amenity rather than an elite privilege.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Howard's garden city idea links planning and green space
description: The garden becomes central to modern thinking about healthier industrial cities.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Victory gardens turn home plots into wartime infrastructure
description: Households grow food at scale to support national supply systems during World War II.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Environmental design reshapes horticulture
description: Ecology, native planting, and conservation influence garden practice more strongly.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Community gardening expands in cities
description: Vacant lots and neighborhoods are repurposed for food growing, civic life, and environmental education.

timeline_description: A history of gardens from ancient irrigated enclosures and cloisters to botanical gardens, public parks, and vertical farms.
title: Vertical and hydroponic farms bring gardens indoors
description: Controlled-environment agriculture turns stacked urban growing into a new model of cultivated space.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Dogs are established as household companions
description: By the early historic era, dogs are widely kept not only for work but also for companionship.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Pet-keeping appears in elite households
description: Small companion animals become visible in elite domestic settings and burials.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Cats become household pets in Egypt
description: Cats move from useful mousers to animals closely associated with domestic affection and ritual value.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Romans popularize companion birds and lapdogs
description: Urban elites in the Roman world keep small animals for pleasure, status, and sentiment.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Courtly falconry and companion keeping expand
description: Elite cultures distinguish between working animals and cherished household companions.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Song China domesticates ornamental goldfish
description: Selective keeping of goldfish turns a managed food species into a decorative household pet.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Canaries enter European homes
description: Imported songbirds become popular domestic pets and symbols of cultivated leisure.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Toy dog breeding gains prestige
description: Small companion breeds become strongly associated with courtly and aristocratic life.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Pet-keeping broadens beyond elites
description: Companion animals become more common in middle-class households as domestic culture changes.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: The RSPCA reflects new concern for animal welfare
description: Organized animal protection reshapes how companion animals are treated in public life.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Modern cat shows begin
description: Public exhibitions help turn pedigree cats into recognized and marketable companion animals.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: The Kennel Club standardizes dog breeds
description: Formal breed registries reshape breeding, ownership, and the modern idea of pedigree pets.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Commercial pet food emerges
description: Industrial production changes how urban households feed companion animals.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Veterinary care becomes a routine part of pet ownership
description: Household pets increasingly receive specialized medical attention rather than only ad hoc treatment.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Suburban pet culture expands
description: Rising incomes and family-oriented housing fuel mass pet ownership after World War II.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Microchipping improves pet identification
description: Electronic identification becomes an important tool for reuniting lost animals with owners.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Tamagotchi launches the digital pet
description: A handheld virtual creature turns care, attachment, and routine feeding into electronic play.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Designer breeds become a consumer phenomenon
description: Crossbred companion dogs are marketed for lifestyle appeal as pet culture becomes more commercialized.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Pet tech enters everyday care
description: Apps, GPS trackers, smart feeders, and connected cameras turn pet care into a digital service ecosystem.

timeline_description: A history of pets from early companion animals and ornamental species to breed culture, pet consumerism, and digital pets.
title: Companion animals are treated as family members
description: High spending on health, enrichment, and services marks the mature era of the pampered pet.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: Woolly mammoths survive only in relict populations
description: The last isolated mammoth populations persist into the late Holocene before disappearing.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: Moa disappear after human settlement
description: New Zealand's giant flightless birds vanish within a few centuries of Polynesian settlement.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The dodo is first recorded by Europeans
description: Human contact begins the short terminal phase for one of history's most famous island species.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The dodo disappears
description: Within decades of human arrival, hunting and introduced species drive the dodo to extinction.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: Steller's sea cow vanishes
description: An enormous marine mammal is hunted to extinction only 27 years after scientific description.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The bluebuck disappears
description: The bluebuck becomes one of the first large African mammals driven to extinction in modern times.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The great auk is lost
description: The extinction of the flightless North Atlantic bird becomes an early conservation warning.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The quagga disappears
description: The zebra relative vanishes in the wild and captivity amid colonial hunting pressure.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: Martha, the last passenger pigeon, dies
description: The death of the last known passenger pigeon symbolizes industrial-era overexploitation.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The last heath hen dies
description: The extinction of the heath hen underscores the fragility of remnant populations.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The last known thylacine dies in captivity
description: The thylacine becomes a defining case of modern human-caused extinction.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The IUCN is founded
description: Global conservation gains a durable institution for assessing species risk and extinction threat.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The Red List era begins
description: Systematic status assessment helps turn extinction risk into a global scientific and policy framework.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: CITES targets trade-driven species loss
description: An international treaty links wildlife commerce to extinction prevention.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The 'sixth extinction' enters mainstream science
description: Scientists increasingly describe a modern extinction wave driven by habitat loss and overuse.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: The Pyrenean ibex is declared extinct
description: The loss of the subspecies later becomes central to de-extinction debates.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: A cloned Pyrenean ibex is briefly born
description: The short-lived clone becomes a landmark experiment in de-extinction science.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: Western black rhinoceros is declared extinct
description: The announcement highlights the continuing loss of large mammals in the modern era.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: Colossal launches a mammoth revival effort
description: A high-profile private initiative popularizes de-extinction as a biotech and conservation project.

timeline_description: A history of extinction from mass die-offs and iconic species losses to modern conservation and de-extinction science.
title: Extinction prevention and de-extinction coexist uneasily
description: Modern conservation increasingly weighs species recovery, genetic rescue, and the ethics of resurrection science.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Sugarcane is cultivated in New Guinea
description: Early cultivation of sugarcane begins the long human history of concentrated sweetness.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Sugarcane spreads through Southeast Asia
description: Human migration and exchange move cane cultivation into new tropical regions.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: India develops sugar crystallization
description: Boiling cane juice into crystals creates a portable sweetener later traded across Eurasia.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Greek writers note 'honey without bees'
description: Mediterranean observers encounter reports of crystallized sugar from India.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Islamic expansion spreads cane and refining
description: Sugar cultivation and processing move widely across the Middle East and Mediterranean.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Sugar reaches crusader and Mediterranean markets
description: European demand grows as sugar appears as a luxury good in elite consumption.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Atlantic islands become sugar laboratories
description: Portuguese and Spanish plantations on islands anticipate later Caribbean plantation systems.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Sugar plantations reshape the Caribbean and Brazil
description: Large-scale plantation agriculture ties sugar to slavery, empire, and global trade.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Sugar becomes a driver of the triangular trade
description: European consumption links plantation sugar to enslaved labor and Atlantic finance.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Tea, coffee, and chocolate deepen sugar demand
description: Sweetened beverages help normalize daily sugar consumption in Europe.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Britain passes the Sugar Act
description: Imperial taxation of molasses and sugar becomes a flashpoint in colonial America.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: The Haitian Revolution disrupts the sugar order
description: The world's richest sugar colony is transformed by slave revolt and independence.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Beet sugar rises in continental Europe
description: Political disruption and war encourage large-scale sugar beet cultivation and processing.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Refining lowers prices and broadens access
description: Industrial processing helps turn sugar from a luxury into a mass commodity.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Confectionery becomes industrialized
description: Cheap sugar supports candy, jam, and mass processed foods for urban consumers.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Sugar saturates processed-food systems
description: Soft drinks, packaged foods, and industrial formulations drive historically high sugar intake.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Nutrition debates sharpen around sugar and fat
description: Public-health disputes begin reshaping how sugar is discussed in relation to chronic disease.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: High-fructose sweeteners expand in industry
description: New sweetener economics change beverage and processed-food manufacturing.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: WHO issues updated free-sugars guidance
description: Global health guidance urges lower sugar intake to reduce obesity and dental disease risk.

timeline_description: A history of sugar from cane cultivation and crystallization to plantation empires, industrial sweeteners, and modern public-health policy.
title: Sugar policy becomes a public-health battleground
description: Taxes, labeling, and reformulation efforts reflect the mature politics of sugar consumption.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Village middens concentrate household waste
description: Early settlements accumulate refuse in designated dumping areas outside daily living space.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Rome manages urban refuse at scale
description: Large cities confront collection, dumping, and sanitation as persistent civic problems.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Late medieval towns appoint scavengers
description: Authorities increasingly regulate street filth and waste hauling outside city walls.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: English cities require official scavengers
description: Municipal waste collection becomes a more regular public responsibility.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Industrial cities multiply refuse volumes
description: Urban growth and manufacturing intensify disposal and sanitation challenges.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: The first modern municipal incinerator opens
description: Controlled burning enters the toolkit of urban waste disposal.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Urban sanitation becomes a reform priority
description: Public-health movements push cities toward more systematic waste handling.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Sanitary landfill is introduced in England
description: Layered, controlled land disposal replaces many open dumping practices.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Consumer packaging accelerates waste growth
description: Mass production and packaged goods create new streams of household rubbish.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Plastics transform the waste stream
description: Cheap synthetic materials create durable waste with long environmental persistence.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: The modern recycling movement gains traction
description: Reclaiming paper, metals, and glass becomes part of environmental politics.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Earth Day mainstreams waste awareness
description: Waste and pollution become central public concerns in the new environmental movement.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Hazardous waste regulation expands
description: Governments increasingly distinguish toxic wastes from ordinary municipal refuse.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Curbside recycling becomes common
description: Household sorting and municipal pickup make recycling a routine civic practice in many cities.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: The Great Pacific Garbage Patch enters public view
description: Oceanic plastic accumulation becomes a defining symbol of global waste leakage.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: E-waste becomes a major global issue
description: Discarded electronics expose the waste consequences of rapid technological turnover.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Zero-waste and circular-economy thinking spread
description: Attention shifts from disposal alone to redesign, reuse, repair, and materials recovery.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Basel parties adopt plastic waste amendments
description: Cross-border trade in many plastic wastes comes under stronger international control.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Plastic waste amendments take effect
description: New Basel rules bring many plastic waste shipments under formal international procedures.

timeline_description: A history of waste from early middens and municipal scavengers to plastics, ocean debris, recycling, and the circular economy.
title: Circularity becomes the leading waste horizon
description: Policy and industry increasingly frame waste as a design failure rather than an inevitable endpoint.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Oil lamps illuminate early cities
description: Stone and ceramic lamps allow controlled indoor light after sunset in ancient urban societies.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Rushlights and candle-like devices appear
description: Fat-soaked plant materials provide portable artificial light in ancient households.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Romans popularize wicked candles
description: Portable candles become more practical for domestic, religious, and public illumination.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Night watch and curfew structure urban darkness
description: Cities organize social life around limited artificial light, patrols, and restricted nocturnal movement.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Lantern culture expands in towns
description: Portable enclosed lighting makes streets, travel, and domestic work after dark somewhat safer.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Argand's oil lamp improves brightness and stability
description: A circular wick and glass chimney greatly improve lamp efficiency and flame quality.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Gas lighting is first demonstrated
description: Manufactured gas begins the transition from flame-based domestic light to urban utility networks.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Davy demonstrates electric incandescence
description: Electricity begins to enter the long story of pushing back darkness.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Gas street lighting reaches London
description: Urban nights start to change as streets become brighter and more navigable.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: The first incandescent lamp patent is granted
description: Frederick de Moleyns patents an early incandescent design, an important step toward practical bulbs.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Kerosene lamps become household standards
description: Cheap petroleum lighting spreads dependable evening light far beyond elite homes.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Arc lamps begin large-scale public lighting
description: Electric arc lighting shows that city streets and factories can be lit at industrial scale.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Practical incandescent lamps arrive
description: Swan and Edison make electric domestic lighting commercially viable.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Mercury-arc lamps improve industrial illumination
description: Electric discharge lighting increases efficiency for factories and streets.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Fluorescent lighting enters common use
description: Bright, efficient artificial light reshapes offices, schools, and commercial interiors.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Visible LEDs are demonstrated
description: Semiconductor light sources open the path toward compact, long-lived illumination.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: High-quality blue LEDs are achieved
description: Breakthroughs in gallium nitride make efficient white LED lighting possible.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Commercial blue LED production begins
description: Industry starts scaling the blue devices crucial for practical white LED lamps.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: Blue LED inventors win the Nobel Prize
description: The award marks LED lighting as one of the key technologies redefining the human night.

timeline_description: A history of how humans pushed back darkness, from early lamps and candles to gaslight, electric bulbs, and LEDs.
title: LEDs dominate the modern illuminated night
description: Highly efficient solid-state lighting becomes the global standard for homes, screens, and cities.
